achievements: High-performance web application with server-side rendering
project.description: Created a performant front-end for an Amplify-hosted web application using Next.js. The platform features server-side rendering for optimal performance and is deployed on AWS Amplify for streamlined hosting and CI/CD.
project.title: Performant Web Application on AWS Amplify
project.technicalApproach: Built the application with Next.js and TypeScript, leveraging server-side rendering for performance. Deployed on AWS Amplify with integrated hosting and continuous deployment pipelines.

achievements: Full multitenant HR platform supporting multiple organizations
Integrated video interviewing and resume parsing capabilities
Streamlined candidate workflows from application to hire
project.description: Built a multitenant human resources application that integrates video interviewing, resume parsing, and candidate workflows. The platform supports multiple organizations on a single deployment with tenant isolation, enabling end-to-end recruitment process automation.
project.title: Multitenant HR Platform with Video Interviewing
project.technicalApproach: Developed a React-based frontend with AWS serverless backend. Integrated video interviewing capabilities, AI-powered resume parsing, and candidate workflow management. Built with multi-tenancy support for organizational isolation.

matchedRequirements: Perform process discovery as needed to automate workflow approvals ensuring timely completion based on Workday or ServiceNow requested effective dates

achievements: Complete infrastructure migration from Digital Ocean to AWS
Full backend overhaul improving performance and security
project.description: Migrated Chanced.com from Digital Ocean to AWS and performed a complete overhaul of their PHP Laravel backend. The project modernized the hosting infrastructure and improved application performance and reliability.
project.title: Digital Ocean to AWS Migration & Backend Overhaul
project.technicalApproach: Migrated hosting from Digital Ocean to AWS. Overhauled the entire PHP Laravel backend for improved performance, security, and maintainability. Leveraged AWS services for hosting, storage, and compute.

matchedRequirements: Leverage existing security group schema to maintain system and resource access and authorization levels for integrated and third-party applications
Complete and submit Attachment E: Technology Security Assessment

Provide evidence of insurance meeting all required coverage limits: General Liability $1M, Auto Liability $1M, Workers' Comp per CA law, Employers' Liability $1M, Professional Liability $1M, Cyber Liability $5M Privacy/Network Security and $1M Technology E&O

achievements: Secure, localized trading platform with CMS-driven content
Automated CI/CD pipeline with Azure Pipelines
Global performance optimization with Cloudflare CDN
project.description: Built a secure cryptocurrency trading platform frontend with dynamic, localized content. The platform features server-side rendering, CMS-driven content management, and CDN optimization for global performance.
project.title: Secure Crypto Trading Platform
project.technicalApproach: Developed the frontend using Next.js with Strapi CMS for dynamic content management. Set up CI/CD with Azure Pipelines for automated deployments. Applied Cloudflare optimizations for global performance and security.

achievements: Automated translation of safety manuals from German to multiple languages
Eliminated manual translation and formatting workflows
Maintained document formatting integrity across languages
project.description: Built an automated transcription and translation system for safety manuals, converting German-language PDF articles into every required country-specific language. The platform ingests PDFs, extracts text, translates content, and regenerates formatted PDF output.
project.title: Multilingual Safety Manual Translation Platform
project.technicalApproach: Used Amazon Textract to extract text from PDF safety manuals. Integrated Amazon Translate for multilingual conversion. Built an automated pipeline to reconvert translated text back into formatted PDF documents.

matchedRequirements: Automate workflow approval notifications for onboarding/offboarding/transfers and route required approvals for application access or elevated rights requests
Leverage existing security group schema to maintain system and resource access and authorization levels for integrated and third-party applications
Provide a phased system and function delivery approach that is adaptive and agile to minimize user and system access disruptions
Provide documentation on system administration and change processes to maintain automation regardless of changing system of record processes

recommendations: 39 critical gap(s) identified. Consider teaming arrangements or subcontractors with relevant experience.
Overall past performance coverage is below 70%. This may significantly impact win probability.
criticalGaps: Assess Active Directory and Entra ID Sync: validate readiness for IAM automation and SSO replacement, analyze AD settings for best practice and secure design, evaluate domain/OU structure, RBAC/delegation, and security group strategy
Identify and remove stale objects and lifecycle inconsistencies in Active Directory
Ensure the identity ecosystem (Entra/AD) is configured to support IAM in alignment with best practice synchronization designs
Replace current Okta SSO with Entra SSO, migrating all currently integrated applications (~400 SAML 2.0 apps) with minimal service interruptions
Migrate all user presentation and authentication processes to Entra SSO with minimal service interruptions or user interaction
Implement IAM automation for workforce onboarding, offboarding, transfers, and system/resource access change assignments supporting both County employees and contractor/vendor lifecycles
Integrate IAM with Workday for employee lifecycle management (system of record for onboarding, offboarding, transfers)
Integrate IAM with ServiceNow for contractor/vendor on/offboarding and application access changes for all workforce members
Automate application provisioning: assign basic application access to new or transferred users determined by department and/or position classification
Automate workflow approval notifications for onboarding/offboarding/transfers and route required approvals for application access or elevated rights requests
Provide a platform/portal to easily request and automate user system access changes including opening, processing, and closing ServiceNow ticket requests
Implement seamless MFA migration from existing SSO (Okta) to new Entra SSO
Implement SAML SSO integration for external partners while minimizing access disruptions
Leverage existing security group schema to maintain system and resource access and authorization levels for integrated and third-party applications

Support cross-domain workforce member transfers: maintain same account name and data stores, same account name without previous data store access, or new account name without previous data store access
Enforce contractor account expiration dates with automated option to extend with requisite approvals and authorizations
Provide escalation capability for approval requests that are expired or left unattended
Perform process discovery as needed to automate workflow approvals ensuring timely completion based on Workday or ServiceNow requested effective dates
Resolve any functional Active Directory synchronization issues that might hinder or overcomplicate the IAM and SSO objectives
Leverage all current County Microsoft licensing levels (predominantly Microsoft 365 G3) whenever possible to reduce costs on third-party platforms
Provide a phased system and function delivery approach that is adaptive and agile to minimize user and system access disruptions
Provide methodology for stakeholders to have visibility into the progress of on/offboarding, transfer, and application change requests
Provide additional protections to maintain least privilege access based on data/application owner authorized levels
Audit and report on existing on-prem environment against best practices to identify orphaned accounts or resource objects for removal
Complete and submit Attachment E: Technology Security Assessment
Proposer must be registered, active, and in good standing with SAM.gov and not barred from public contracting by any federal or state authority

Provide evidence of insurance meeting all required coverage limits: General Liability $1M, Auto Liability $1M, Workers' Comp per CA law, Employers' Liability $1M, Professional Liability $1M, Cyber Liability $5M Privacy/Network Security and $1M Technology E&O
Fingerprinting and background checks may be required at the discretion of the County based on project requirements
Proposer has minimum three (3) years' experience implementing the proposed Microsoft IAM solution
Proposer has minimum three (3) successful implementations for government agencies of similar size and requirements within the last five (5) years
The successful proposer will be singularly responsible for the proposed solution and will not use subcontractors to perform the services
Provide titles and names of staff members on the project team and expected availability of individuals
Provide operational runbooks for IAM and SSO administration
Provide architecture diagrams documenting the implemented solution
Conduct knowledge transfer sessions to fully transfer support knowledge to County TSD workforce members
Create Knowledge Base articles and training materials for County workforce management resources including departmental PRCs
Provide documentation on system administration and change processes to maintain automation regardless of changing system of record processes
Manage and coordinate all communications to minimize impact to system access during SSO migration
Provide at least three references from successfully completed projects of similar nature with government agencies
